taskSummary: Replace vibe-next-template's legacy AI workflow/governance layer with the new GitHub Copilot-first six-phase workflow, then migrate the application structure, conventions, tooling, and docs to the new Next.js enterprise profile without breaking existing behavior.

acceptanceCriteria: The repository removes the previous workflow/governance artifacts and adopts this repository's workflow layer as the only active AI workflow contract.
The application keeps its current user-facing behavior, including locale-prefixed next-intl routing and the existing landing-page experience.
The codebase is migrated toward the new Next.js enterprise profile conventions, especially feature-module structure, naming, routing, i18n, testability, and server-first boundaries.
Repository scripts, linting, docs, MCP config, and validation flows are updated so the app and the workflow can coexist cleanly.
Applicable quality gates pass for the migrated repository, including workflow validation plus app typecheck, lint, tests, and build with a valid example env.
Any remaining workflow gaps or migration blockers discovered during the real-repo test are captured explicitly for follow-up.
constraints: Preserve the existing functional behavior of the template application while replacing the workflow layer 100%.
Keep Next.js 16+, React 19, next-intl en/th locale-prefix routing, and the current dependency baseline unless a change is required for correctness.
Prefer incremental migration slices over a one-shot rewrite so regressions can be isolated and fixed deliberately.
The migrated repository must be usable with GitHub Copilot VS Code, GitHub Copilot CLI, and Codex-based pre-commit auditing.

plan.summary: Migrate in four slices: replace workflow/governance artifacts, align toolchain/docs with the new workflow, refactor application structure into the new feature-module conventions while preserving behavior, then run full gates and capture remaining workflow gaps.

qualityGates.lastRunSummary: typecheck, lint, tests, workflow state validation, workflow repository validation, workflow proof, build:example-env, build:storybook, and git diff --check all passed after replacing the legacy workflow layer, removing the old bin-based workflow helpers, migrating the landing app surface from src/modules/static-pages to src/features/landing, and completing the final landing feature naming cleanup.

delivery.notes: The repository now uses the new six-phase workflow contract end-to-end, application CI covers both workflow and app gates, the landing surface lives under src/features/landing, the legacy container/screen naming has been removed from the active feature tree, and migration findings are documented in docs/migrations/vibe-next-template-workflow-migration-findings.md.
